exploration_log.aha_moment: AssembliesToRegister is already a list that drives all scanning. Adding ScanReferencedAssemblies as a bool flag and extending assembliesToScan before any ConnectImplementationsToTypesClosing calls is the minimal, clean approach.
exploration_log.hindsight: Could have seen the full pattern immediately — both files were needed and both were identified from the task description.

alternatives: Recursively scan all referenced assemblies transitively (deep)
Only scan direct references (one level deep)
key_decisions.decision: Scan one level of referenced assemblies via GetReferencedAssemblies()
key_decisions.reasoning: The task says 'assemblies referenced by the provided assemblies' (singular level). Recursive scanning could include the entire .NET framework, which would be too broad. One level is safe and matches the described use case.

solve_notes: Added a `ScanReferencedAssemblies` bool property to `MediatRServiceConfiguration`. In `ServiceRegistrar.AddMediatRClasses`, when this flag is true, the method loads each referenced assembly via `Assembly.Load` (guarding against load failures) and appends them to `assembliesToScan` before any handler registration takes place.
task_text:
Fix ServiceRegistrar not scanning handlers from referenced assemblies transitively

In `src/MediatR/Registration/ServiceRegistrar.cs`, the assembly scanning
only processes explicitly provided assemblies. Handlers defined in
assemblies referenced by the provided assemblies but not listed
themselves are silently missed. Fix `ServiceRegistrar` to optionally
scan referenced assemblies when configured via
`MediatrServiceConfiguration.ScanReferencedAssemblies`.
